reasoning: The document contains multiple major defects, including incorrectly formatted ordered lists that conflict with the text's non-sequential numbering. Furthermore, both LLM fidelity and programmatic signals strongly corroborate that portions of the text were duplicated as full-page screenshot images alongside the extracted text, accompanied by truncated alt text. With 4 major defects in total, this falls within the threshold for HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: On page 3 of the source document, there is a series of non-sequentially numbered paragraphs identifying specific basins (e.g., 'Basin 1:', 'Basin 2:', 'Basin 4/5:', 'Basin 8:', etc.). The extraction pipeline incorrectly identified this sequence of text as an ordered list ('list_type: "ordered"'). Rendering these items as an HTML ordered list will inject automatic sequential numbering (1, 2, 3, 4...) that directly conflicts with the explicit, non-sequential basin numbers in the text. Unnumbered/c

Basin 2: ...
Basin 3: ...
Basin 4/5: ...
Basin 8: ...
concerns.output_quote: "type": "list", "list_type": "ordered", "items": [ {"text": "Basin 1: The outlet of the slope drain appears to be placed on top of the first baffle and flow into the second cell"}, ...

concerns.analysis: The HTML contains screenshots of portions of the document's text inserted as images alongside the extracted HTML text for those exact same sections. Image 2's alt text describes 'a numbered list of requirements regarding a Mine Map, traffic', which corresponds to the list text immediately above it. Image 3's alt text describes 'referencing regulation 15A NC', which corresponds to the paragraph text extracted just above it on page 4. This results in the user seeing the same content twice: once as

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (3 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ntal Quality, featuring the state seal and listing"; p1: "...list of requirements regarding a Mine Map, traffic"; p4: "... permit application, referencing regulation 15A NC"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=3, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The phrases 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' are underlined in the source PDF. This underline emphasis is lost in the output JSON, which renders them as plain text. The rule specifically states that if the source shows underlined text and the output does not, it is a genuine emphasis loss.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Remove duplicate text screenshot images from the document.
Fix the ordered list formatting for the basins to reflect the original non-sequential numbering without automatic HTML list numbering.
Fix truncated alt text descriptions if images are retained.
